description: Decompilation and code audit of pre-auth Java servlet handlers via CFR decompiler

pre_auth_mode_bypass.description: CyberoamCommonServlet._doPost() at the session check (line 154 equivalent) exempts these modes from session validation. This is the definitive pre-auth bypass list -- broader than what AuthConstants defines.

findings.title: APICommonServlet Pre-Auth File Upload Gated by Configuration (Not Default-Exploitable)
findings.category: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function (conditional)
findings.description: The /webconsole/APIController endpoint accepts pre-auth multipart POST uploads (explicitly exempted by RequestCheckFilter). The APICommonServlet writes user-supplied XML to /sdisk/APIXMLInput/ and executes api_parser via Runtime.getRuntime().exec(). However, sanitizeRequest() checks XmlApiConfigUtils.xmlApiConfigStatus() and validateXmlApiRequest(IP). The XML API is DISABLED by default (tblapiconfig.isenable=false) and requires admin-configured IP allowlisting. This means the endpoint is not exploitable in default configuration.
findings.evidence: DB default: INSERT INTO tblapiconfig (isenable, ipaddresses) VALUES (false, NULL). sanitizeRequest() returns -2 when API disabled. Runtime.exec() of api_parser occurs only after these checks pass.
findings.impact: If an admin enables the XML API and allows the attacker's IP, the attacker can supply arbitrary XML to api_parser without authentication. This could lead to RCE via XML injection into api_parser. NOT exploitable in default deployment.
findings.notes: Score reflects non-default configuration requirement. In practice, many admins enable the XML API for automation. The api_parser binary itself needs further analysis for XML injection / command injection.

findings.title: Pre-Auth Guest User Registration Exposes User Enumeration
findings.description: The pre-auth guest user registration (mode 746) via GuestUserHelper.addGuestUser() checks for duplicate users via checkDuplicateGuestUser(mobileno, ...) and returns status 501 if a user with that mobile number already exists. This allows unauthenticated enumeration of registered guest users by mobile number.
findings.evidence: GuestUserHelper.java: if ((duplicateUserName = GuestUserHelper.checkDuplicateGuestUser(mobileno, ..., sqlReader)) != null) { guestUserName.append(duplicateUserName); status = 501; return status; }
findings.impact: Attacker can enumerate guest user mobile numbers via the pre-auth /webconsole/Controller?mode=746 endpoint. The response differs between existing and non-existing users.

findings.title: X-Forwarded-For Header Unsanitized in Guest User Event Logging
findings.category: CWE-117: Improper Output Neutralization for Logs
findings.description: HFHelper.getClientIP() reads the X-Forwarded-For header with only comma-trimming (no validation). In GuestUserHelper.addGuestUser(), this unsanitized value is concatenated into a ProcessExecutor command argument as 'network.src_ip=<value>'. While the array-based exec prevents direct shell injection, the attacker-controlled value reaches /bin/eventlog as a command-line argument. If eventlog processes this value in a log-injectable way (e.g., writing to syslog, database, or file without sanitization), log injection or log forging is possible.
findings.evidence: HFHelper.getClientIP(): reads X-FORWARDED-FOR, trims at comma, no IP validation. GuestUserHelper line 181: cmdArr.add('network.src_ip=' + HFHelper.getClientIP(request)). ProcessExecutor uses String[] array form (not shell).
findings.impact: Log injection/forging via crafted X-Forwarded-For header. The array-based exec mitigates command injection. Impact depends on how /bin/eventlog processes the src_ip field.

findings.title: Pre-Auth Mode 6000 (validate_hammer_token) -- Sophos Central Auth Bypass Potential
findings.description: Mode 6000 (validate_hammer_token) is accessible pre-auth and appears to handle Sophos Central integration authentication. In CyberoamCommonServlet.doGet(), mode 6000 is handled specially: a new EventBean is created with opcode 'validate_hammer_token' and requestType 2 (CyberoamCustomHelper). This bypasses the normal EventBean.getEventByMode() lookup. If the hammer token validation has weaknesses (predictable tokens, timing attacks, replay), it could allow authentication bypass via Sophos Central SSO.
findings.evidence: CyberoamCommonServlet.doGet(): if (mode == 6000) { eventObject = new EventBean(); eventObject.setMode(mode); eventObject.setOpcode('validate_hammer_token'); eventObject.setRequestType(2); }
findings.impact: Potential authentication bypass via Sophos Central token validation. Requires live testing to determine token validation strength.
findings.notes: NEEDS LIVE TESTING. The validate_hammer_token handler class needs to be identified and decompiled. This is a promising target because SSO/token validation is historically a rich source of auth bypass vulnerabilities.

findings.title: Ancient Java Libraries with Known CVEs
findings.category: CWE-1395: Dependency on Vulnerable Third-Party Component
findings.description: The webconsole webapp includes extremely outdated Java libraries with known CVEs: json-20090211.jar (2009, 17 years old), bcprov-jdk14-138.jar (ancient Bouncy Castle), commons-io-2.2, commons-beanutils-1.9.2, itext-2.1.5. These libraries have known deserialization, path traversal, and cryptographic vulnerabilities.
findings.evidence: WEB-INF/lib/ contains: json-20090211.jar, bcprov-jdk14-138.jar, bcmail-jdk14-138.jar, commons-io-2.2.jar, commons-beanutils-1.9.2.jar, itext-2.1.5.jar, commons-fileupload-1.3.3.jar
findings.impact: Depends on how these libraries are used. commons-beanutils-1.9.2 is exploitable via BeanComparator deserialization gadget (CVE-2019-10086). commons-fileupload has DoS (CVE-2023-24998). json-20090211 may have parsing DoS. Bouncy Castle 138 has numerous cryptographic weaknesses.
findings.notes: The commons-beanutils gadget is only exploitable if Java deserialization of untrusted data occurs somewhere in the application. Need to search for ObjectInputStream usage.

key_observations: The login handler (mode 151) delegates to an internal REST API (access-manager) rather than doing direct SQL auth -- modern architecture
SQL queries in GuestUserHelper use PreparedStatementBuffer consistently -- parameterized queries, no obvious SQLi
ProcessExecutor uses String[] array form -- mitigates command injection via shell metacharacters
The check_bf.sh brute-force protection script uses unquoted shell variables with attacker IP, but the Java-side validIPv4() check prevents exploitation
The ApproveAliases setting in webconsole.xml allows symlink traversal -- potential path traversal if attacker can create symlinks
Mode 6000 (validate_hammer_token) is the most promising unexplored pre-auth vector
next_steps: Decompile the validate_hammer_token handler (CyberoamCustomHelper dispatch for mode 6000)
Search for ObjectInputStream / deserialization usage across all Java classes (commons-beanutils gadget chain)
Analyze /bin/eventlog binary for log injection via the src_ip field
Decompile SSOAdminServlet for SAML/SSO authentication bypass vectors
Analyze api_parser binary for XML injection / command injection
Check Apache httpd ModSecurity ruleset for bypass opportunities
Map the /userportal pre-auth surface (mode 451 login + mode 8100)
Live testing required for validate_hammer_token, guest user registration, and login brute-force protection
